reasoning: Elon Musk won TIME's Person of the Year in 2021. While his role leading DOGE, soaring wealth, and SpaceX achievements make him highly influential in 2025, TIME rarely awards individuals twice within a 4-year span unless they are a re-elected US President. His major political project (DOGE) ended in May 2025, reducing his narrative momentum for a year-end award. The market estimate of 4% accurately reflects his baseline chances as a perennial newsmaker offset by the lack of a defining, novel late-year catalyst.
